findings.criterion: Public Python, CLI, MCP, user, and agent guidance consistently states that force bypasses all gates.
findings.detail: The Python library docstring (`_lib_writes.py:350`) correctly says `force` "bypasses GATES generally — including any gate added in the future," and the MCP notes it is CLI/library-only. However, the CLI `_USAGE` string (`transition.py:43`) says only "--force Bypass the plan-review gate when starting work (open->in_progress)" — naming only the plan-review gate, not all gates. The user guide (`docs/user-guide.md`) contains no mention of `--force` semantics at all. Agent guidance (`AGENTS.md`, `.agents/rules/rebar.md`) similarly omits any mention of force semantics. The guidance is therefore not consistent across all surfaces.
findings.remediation: Update the CLI `_USAGE` string in `transition.py` to say force bypasses all gates (not just the plan-review gate). Add a brief force/invariant note to `docs/user-guide.md` and `AGENTS.md`.
findings.title: CLI, user guide, and agent guidance do not consistently state force bypasses all gates

findings.criterion: Documentation does not hardcode a closed list of current gates as the definition of force behavior.
findings.detail: The CLI `_USAGE` string in `transition.py` line 43 says "--force Bypass the plan-review gate when starting work" — this hardcodes the plan-review gate as the definition of `--force` behavior. Similarly, `docs/cli-reference.md` line 51 says "--force bypasses the plan-review gate" for the `claim` command. Both present `--force` as tied to a specific named gate rather than the stable abstraction of "bypasses all enabled gates." By contrast, the Python library docstring in `_lib_writes.py` already uses the correct future-proof language.
findings.remediation: Update `transition.py` `_USAGE` and `docs/cli-reference.md` to replace the gate-specific wording with "bypasses all enabled gates (including gates added in the future)" with an audit note. Regenerate any golden help files that pin this output.
findings.title: CLI help and cli-reference.md hardcode 'plan-review gate' as the only gate force bypasses

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
